RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE

Plaintiffs' motion for preference was heard and ruled as follows: Motion is granted per C.C.P. 36(d). Trial is January 7, 2013 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for hearing summary motions is January 7, 2013. All summary motions to be filed on regular notices. Plaintiffs must be produced for deposition within 20 days of this hearing for Goodman Lumber and any other defendant who was served in this action after plaintiffs' prior deposition concluded, if any. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than December 10, 2012. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on January 3, 2013. Fact discovery cut-off date is December 21, 2012. Expert discovery cut-off date is January 7, 2013. No later than Oct. 5, 2012, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than Oct. 5, 2012, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further case management conference on Dec. 11, 2012 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5. All counsel attending the conference must have knowledge about the fact of the case, and be present with the representatives who have authority to settle. Any person(s) need to appear by phone must seek approval by the Court prior to the conference. The date of this conference will be determined by the Court. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 regarding the submission of settlement conference statements. The statements will be kept confidential. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)
